content: Idempotent event processing in Postgres: two common mechanisms -- (1) a UNIQUE constraint on a natural idempotency key (e.g. composite of event source + entity id + event type) gives DB-level conflict detection with no extra logic and keeps the dedup check transactionally consistent with the write it protects; (2) an explicit event_id column combined with INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (event_id) DO NOTHING/UPDATE is the standard upsert-based dedup pattern. Both are DB-enforced and equivalent in correctness for single-table, single-process idempotency; the composite natural-key unique constraint is preferable when the identity is inherently derivable from business fields (avoids needing to plumb a separate ID through the event payload).
